leagueBriefing: A dramatic night in the NBA featured an overtime classic between West rivals, a superstar injury in Hollywood, and a red-hot winning streak reaching new heights in Atlanta.

games.headline: Sixers survive massive Nets rally after nearly blowing 28-point lead
games.brief: The 76ers looked like they were cruising to an easy win in Philly, leading by as many as 28 points, but the Nets turned the afternoon into a nail-biter with a furious 41-point fourth quarter. Brooklyn actually took a late lead before Quentin Grimes steadied the ship for a short-handed Sixers squad missing their biggest stars.
bullets: Quentin Grimes led the way for Philadelphia with a season-high 28 points and 8 rebounds.
The Nets trailed 67-39 in the third quarter but used a late 3-point barrage to briefly lead in the final minutes.
Philadelphia played without Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey, and Paul George.
Brooklyn’s Danny Wolf notched a double-double with 15 points and 10 boards in the loss.
games.context: The win helps Philadelphia maintain its playoff positioning while Brooklyn continues to struggle with 13 losses in their last 15 games.

highlights.title: NETS at 76ERS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026

games.headline: McCollum and Johnson fuel Hawks to ninth straight victory
games.brief: The Hawks are officially the hottest team in the league after dismantling the Bucks to extend their winning streak to nine games. CJ McCollum was lights-out from deep, and Jalen Johnson continued his breakout campaign with another triple-double, leaving a struggling Milwaukee team with no answers in the second half.
bullets: CJ McCollum exploded for 30 points, including seven makes from beyond the arc.
Jalen Johnson recorded his ninth triple-double of the season with 23 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds.
The nine-game streak is Atlanta's longest since their franchise-record 19-game run in 2014-15.
Ryan Rollins led the Bucks with 22 points, but Milwaukee has now lost eight of its last nine.
games.context: Atlanta's surge has them climbing the Eastern Conference standings, while Milwaukee's freefall continues.

highlights.title: BUCKS at HAWKS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026

games.headline: Spurs dominate from start to finish to stifle Hornets in San Antonio
games.brief: San Antonio protected home court with a wire-to-wire performance against Charlotte. While the Hornets made a brief push in the fourth quarter to cut the deficit to single digits, the Spurs never truly lost control, relying on a balanced attack to pull away late and secure the comfortable double-digit win.
bullets: LaMelo Ball moved into 6th place on the Hornets' all-time scoring list during the game.
The Spurs led by as many as 14 points and successfully thwarted a late Charlotte comeback attempt.
Charlotte struggled to find offensive rhythm, trailing for the vast majority of the contest.
The victory bolsters the Spurs' impressive 26-7 record at home this season.
games.context: The Spurs continue to look like a formidable threat in the West, especially when playing at the Frost Bank Center.

highlights.title: HORNETS at SPURS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026

games.headline: Queta's big night powers Celtics as Wizards' skid hits 11
games.brief: The Celtics got back into the win column by taking care of business against the reeling Wizards. A dominant 35-14 second quarter broke the game open early, and while Washington managed to trim a 30-point deficit down to 11 late, the result was never in doubt as Boston dominated the glass and the paint.
bullets: Neemias Queta had a career night with 24 points and 10 rebounds.
Jayson Tatum contributed a versatile 20 points, 14 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Boston dominated the boards, outrebounding Washington 55-38, including 19 offensive rebounds.
The Wizards have now lost 11 straight games and 9 in a row on the road.
games.context: Boston snaps a two-game losing streak to stay in the hunt for the East's top seed, while Washington remains mired in a season-long slump.

highlights.title: WIZARDS at CELTICS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026

games.headline: Magic sweep season series over Heat to extend winning streak to seven
games.brief: In a battle of Florida rivals, the Magic proved they currently own the state by holding off a late Heat rally to complete a 5-0 season sweep. Paolo Banchero was the orchestrator for an Orlando team that led by 22 but had to lean on clutch free throws in the final seconds to snap Miami's seven-game winning streak.
bullets: Paolo Banchero led all scorers with 27 points, 8 rebounds, and 7 assists.
The Magic have won seven games in a row and finished the season series 5-0 against Miami.
Jaime Jaquez Jr. paced the Heat with 22 points off the bench in the comeback attempt.
Orlando won despite missing key rotation players Franz Wagner and Anthony Black.
games.context: Orlando's dominance of the Heat this season secures a potential tiebreaker advantage in a crowded Eastern Conference playoff race.

games.headline: Doncic hits OT winner as Lakers edge Nuggets in thriller
games.brief: In what felt like a playoff preview, the Lakers outlasted the Nuggets in an overtime instant classic at Crypto.com Arena. After Austin Reaves forced the extra period with a clutch sequence, Luka Doncic delivered the final blow with a game-winning bucket with just 0.5 seconds left in OT, capping a massive comeback for Los Angeles.
bullets: Luka Doncic recorded a triple-double with 30 points, 11 rebounds, and 13 assists.
Austin Reaves scored a team-high 32 points, including the free throws to force overtime.
Nikola Jokic countered with 22 points, 16 rebounds, and 14 assists in the loss.
41-year-old LeBron James drew massive praise for a late-game dive for a loose ball to secure a key possession.
games.context: The win propels the Lakers into the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference and marks their fifth straight victory.

highlights.title: NUGGETS at LAKERS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026

games.headline: Westbrook's triple-double leads Kings as Leonard exits with injury
games.brief: Russell Westbrook turned back the clock against his former team, recording a historic triple-double to lead the Kings to a road win in Inglewood. However, the game was overshadowed by a concerning injury to Kawhi Leonard, who left in the fourth quarter with a sprained ankle after setting a new franchise record for consecutive 20-point games.
bullets: Russell Westbrook tallied his 209th career triple-double with 12 points, 12 rebounds, and 10 assists.
Kawhi Leonard scored 31 points before exiting, marking his 45th straight game with 20+ points (a franchise record).
DeMar DeRozan led Sacramento in scoring with 27 points on efficient 11-of-14 shooting.
The Kings led by as many as 20 points before surviving a Leonard-less Clippers rally in the fourth.
games.context: The severity of Leonard's ankle injury will be the primary focus for the Clippers as they head into the final month of the season.

highlights.title: KINGS at CLIPPERS | FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS | March 14, 2026
